heading: Surveillance requirements
label: 4901 Surveillance requirements
notes: References in TextEnactment of the Don Young Coast Guard Authorization Act of 2022, referred to in subsec. (c)(1), means enactment of div. K of , which was approved .Pub. L. 117–263Dec. 23, 2022

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: A vessel engaged in commercial service that does not carry passengers, shall maintain a video surveillance system.

children.label: A
children.text: on a voyage of at least 600 miles and crosses seaward of the Boundary Line; or

children.label: B
children.text: at least 24 meters (79 feet) in overall length and required to have a load line under chapter 51;

documented vessels of at least 500 gross tons as measured under section 14502, or an alternate tonnage measured under section 14302 as prescribed by the Secretary under section 14104 on an international voyage; and

vessels with overnight accommodations for at least 10 individuals on board that are operating for no less than 72 hours on waters superjacent to the outer Continental Shelf (as defined in section 2(a) of the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act ().43 U.S.C. 1331(a)1So in original. Another closing parenthesis probably should precede the period.1

children.heading: Placement of Video and Audio Surveillance Equipment.—
children.label: c Placement of Video and Audio Surveillance Equipment.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The owner of a vessel to which this section applies shall install video and audio surveillance equipment aboard the vessel not later than 2 years after enactment of the Don Young Coast Guard Authorization Act of 2022, or during the next scheduled drydock, whichever is later.

children.heading: .—Locations
children.label: 2 .—Locations
children.text: Video and audio surveillance equipment shall be placed in passageways on to which doors from staterooms open. Such equipment shall be placed in a manner ensuring the visibility of every door in each such passageway.

children.heading: .—Notice of Video and Audio Surveillance
children.label: d .—Notice of Video and Audio Surveillance
children.text: The owner of a vessel to which this section applies shall provide clear and conspicuous signs on board the vessel notifying the crew of the presence of video and audio surveillance equipment.

children.heading: .—Access to Video and Audio Records
children.label: e .—Access to Video and Audio Records
children.text: The owner of a vessel to which this section applies shall ensure that access to records of video and audio surveillance is not used as part of a labor action against a crew member or employment dispute unless used in a criminal or civil action.

children.heading: .—Retention Requirements
children.label: f .—Retention Requirements
children.text: The owner of a vessel to which this section applies shall retain all records of audio and video surveillance for not less than 1 year after the footage is obtained. Any video and audio surveillance found to be associated with an alleged incident should be preserved for not less than 5 years from the date of the alleged incident.

children.label: A
children.text: retain audio and visual records and other evidence objectively; and

children.label: B
children.text: act impartially without influence from the company or others; and
training on applicable Federal, State, Tribal, and local laws and regulations regarding sexual assault and sexual harassment investigations and reporting requirements.

children.heading: .—Definition of Owner
children.label: g .—Definition of Owner
children.text: In this section, the term “owner” means the owner, charterer, managing operator, master, or other individual in charge of a vessel.

children.heading: .—Exemption
children.label: h .—Exemption
children.text: Fishing vessels, fish processing vessels, and fish tender vessels are exempt from this section.
